purpose: Keep Canvas subsystem documentation thin, routed and split by boundary so no single Markdown file becomes a mixed rule dump.
principle: Canvas documentation follows the root AGENTS.md pattern: the baseline document is a thin entrypoint, machine-readable JSON files remain the system truth, and detailed human explanations are routed through this docs map.

baseline_doc.role: thin human entrypoint for the Canvas subsystem

machine_truth.role: Canvas subsystem policy, responsibilities, view levels, layout protocol and quality gates
Canonical Canvas view registry, storage layout, view boundaries, status and source binding
Layout-free machine navigation graph for Canvas views
Canonical map for Canvas subsystem documentation boundaries
Canonical map for required, shared, deferred and not-applicable visual coverage decisions

doc_routes.boundary: Only explains where to start and which document owns which topic.

doc_routes.role: Human explanation of Canvas subsystem architecture
doc_routes.boundary: Explains why Canvas exists as a visual architecture operating layer and how it connects to machine truth.

doc_routes.role: Human guide for Canvas file creation, directory placement, naming and lifecycle
doc_routes.boundary: Explains where Canvas files live and how they are registered.

doc_routes.role: Human guide for Canvas view level, category, primary subject and not_for boundaries
doc_routes.boundary: Explains what a Canvas view is allowed to express.

doc_routes.role: Human guide for machine-readable versus human-readable Canvas data
doc_routes.boundary: Explains why .canvas layout data is not machine truth.

doc_routes.role: Human visual review record and visual quality rationale
doc_routes.boundary: Records audit findings and layout improvement decisions.

doc_routes.role: Human operation guide for opening and browsing Canvas in Obsidian
doc_routes.boundary: Explains local Obsidian usage and common viewing issues.

split_policy.baseline_doc_rule: The baseline document stays thin. If a rule needs more than a short pointer, move it to the owning doc route and register it here.
split_policy.one_primary_topic_rule: Each Canvas documentation file should have one primary topic and an explicit boundary.
split_policy.machine_truth_rule: Markdown is human explanation. Behavior, routing, status, placement, navigation and validation truth must live in JSON registries or scripts.
split_policy.growth_trigger: When a Canvas documentation topic grows into multiple subtopics, create a new docs/canvas/*.md file and add a doc_routes entry before relying on it.
